settings.title: A New Hot Stone Massage Joins the Menu
settings.excerpt: Smooth basalt stones and slow strokes, for the kind of tension a normal massage cannot reach.
settings.body:
We have added something we are excited about. Velvet Touch now offers a ninety-minute hot stone massage, and it is the deepest relaxation on our menu.

We spent a while getting it right before putting it on the list. The stones, the timing, and the heat all had to be just so, because done carelessly a hot stone treatment is more gimmick than therapy.

How it works

Warmed basalt stones rest along the back and are used to work the muscles, so the heat sinks in before the pressure does. It reaches tension that ordinary kneading tends to skim over.

Basalt holds heat steadily and evenly, which is why we use it. The warmth lets the muscle soften first, so the therapist can work deeper without you bracing against it.

Who it suits

It is best for tight shoulders and the kind of stress that lives in the neck. A few things worth knowing before you book:

  • Come a little early so you are not rushing in tense.
  • Drink water afterwards, as you would after any deep massage.
  • Tell your therapist about any areas to ease off or focus on.

Book it as a standalone or pair it with a facial for a longer afternoon. Either way, plan to take it slowly when you leave. This is not a treatment to rush back to a desk from.
